stories.headline: Aave Bleeds $9B in Outflows as Kelp Contagion Rips Through DeFi Lending
stories.tldr: Aave net inflows collapsed by roughly $6.2B (~23%) after the Kelp exploiter used stolen rsETH as collateral to borrow $195M in ETH.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: One cross-chain bridge failure just triggered the biggest confidence shock to on-chain lending since Terra — bad debt at the base layer of DeFi is back on the table.
the_details: Kelp attacker borrowed ~$195M in ETH on Aave against drained rsETH, leaving bad debt stranded on the lending market.
Aave saw ~$9B in gross outflows and a $6.2B (23%) decline in net inflows in the 24 hours after the exploit.
Contagion spread beyond Aave as lenders re-price rsETH and other LRT collateral across every major money market.

stories.headline: Vercel Breach Sends Crypto Devs Scrambling to Rotate API Keys Site-Wide
stories.tldr: Vercel disclosed a security incident that may have exposed customer API keys, forcing crypto projects hosted on the platform to rotate credentials and audit code.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Most front-ends in crypto ride on Vercel — a single infra breach can silently weaponize RPC keys, indexer tokens, and admin panels across hundreds of dapps.
the_details: Vercel confirmed attackers may have accessed environment variables and API keys tied to customer projects.
Reports peg an associated ransom demand at ~$2M, per CoinCentral coverage of the incident.
Crypto teams are being told to rotate RPC, Alchemy, Infura, and third-party keys and audit deploys made during the breach window.

stories.headline: Crypto Market Cap Slides 1.6% to $2.59T as BTC Drops Under $75K
stories.tldr: Total crypto market cap fell to $2.59T with BTC at $74,547 (-1.41%); fear sentiment is holding as the Kelp and Vercel shocks reprice risk.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A sub-$75K Bitcoin print during a DeFi security cycle is where leveraged longs get flushed — watch funding and basis before calling a bottom.
the_details: BTC trades at $74,547, down 1.41% on $32.4B of 24h volume; dominance remains elevated at 57.4%.
ETH holds ~10.6% dominance; total 24h spot volume across crypto printed $116.82B.
Polkadot and XRP Ledger ecosystem tokens led the day's gainers against a broadly red tape.

stories.headline: Crypto Billionaire Offers Direct Talks to Suspected NK-Linked Kelp Hacker
stories.tldr: A prominent crypto billionaire publicly offered to negotiate directly with the North Korea-linked attacker behind the $290M Kelp DAO exploit to recover stranded rsETH.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Open negotiation with a sanctioned state actor is a legal minefield — OFAC exposure could reach anyone touching whitehat bounty flows.
the_details: The billionaire proposed direct talks after the Kelp attacker drained 116,500 rsETH across 20 chains via a LayerZero bridge flaw.
Researchers link the exploit to the same DPRK-affiliated cluster tied to April's $285M Drift attack.
Any ransom or bounty routed to sanctioned addresses risks OFAC penalties for intermediaries, including bridges and mixers.

stories.headline: BIO Protocol Rips 48%, DeXe Adds 22% as Altcoin Pockets Defy Extreme Fear
stories.tldr: BIO Protocol jumped 48.56% and DeXe climbed 22% to $12.19 even as the CMC Altcoin Season Index sits at 34/100 in deep Bitcoin Season.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Narrative rotations are still paying in Extreme Fear — DeSci and governance infra tokens are getting bid while the broader tape bleeds.
the_details: BIO Protocol topped Upbit volume charts on April 16 with a 48.56% single-day ramp.
DeXe (DEXE) broke $12.19 on April 15, now +386% since February 2026.
Altcoin Season Index at 34/100; BTC dominance near 58.5% keeps most rotations short and sharp.
